report.headline: Heat and pressure swings under a quiet ground hum
report.quick_read: Mid-Atlantic and Ohio–Tennessee face heat with quick shifts; the Mountain West mixes heat with pressure swings; Andean South America sees sharp pressure and temperature changes; East Asia contends with poor air and swings. Space weather sits at a moderate background but is quiet right now. Earth resonances are steady, and the ultra‑low‑frequency field is quiet.

regional_watch.selection_method: At least two fixed anchors must support the same driver.
regional_watch.copy:
Mid-Atlantic US: Heat and humidity with rapid temperature shifts were sampled near New York, Philadelphia, and Pittsburgh. Some people may notice fatigue or sleep disruption.

Ohio and Tennessee Valleys: Heat with rapid temperature shifts was sampled near Cincinnati, Louisville, and Nashville. Fatigue or sleep disruption may stand out for some people.

Mountain West US: Heat combines with pressure swings sampled near Denver, Salt Lake City, and Boise. Some people may notice lower exercise tolerance or head/sinus pressure.

Andean South America: Pressure swings with rapid temperature changes were sampled near Quito, Lima, and La Paz. Some people may notice head or sinus pressure.

East Asia: Poor air quality with some pressure swings was sampled near Beijing, Seoul, and Tokyo. Breathing irritation or headache may be part of what some people notice.

space_watch.copy: Geomagnetic activity sits at a moderate daily background (daily Kp peak ~3.7 with southerly Bz dips), while current conditions are quieter (Kp ~1, Bz near -1.7, solar wind ~433 km/s, density ~4 cm³). A catalog count of CMEs alone does not establish Earth-directed impact today.

earth_signal.health_language: Possible effects may use 'may', 'can', or 'some people notice' without an appended disclaimer.
earth_signal.copy: Schumann resonance fundamentals were detected near 7.37 Hz with harmonics around 14.37 and 21.83 Hz. The ultra-low-frequency geomagnetic field remained quiet and coherent at North American stations BOU and CMO.

items.title: Orange forest fire notification in France

major_events.selection_method: Fresh yellow-or-higher GDACS/USGS events, deduplicated and capped.
major_events.copy:
- France: Orange forest fire notification (started 2026-07-14).
- 34 km WSW of Sarangani, Philippines: Yellow earthquake alert (started 2026-07-14).

platform_copy.facebook:
If the air feels heavy or the day flips from hot to cooler fast, today’s signals line up with that experience.

Quick read: Mid-Atlantic and Ohio–Tennessee are hot with rapid shifts; the Mountain West blends heat with pressure swings; Andean South America has sharp pressure and temperature changes; East Asia contends with poor air and some pressure movement. Space weather is moderate overall but quieter at the moment. Earth resonances are steady with a quiet ULF field.

Regional Watch
- Mid-Atlantic US: Heat and humidity with rapid temperature shifts were sampled around New York, Philadelphia, and Pittsburgh. Some people may notice fatigue or sleep disruption.
- Ohio and Tennessee Valleys: Heat with quick temperature changes was sampled near Cincinnati, Louisville, and Nashville. Fatigue or sleep disruption may stand out for some people.
- Mountain West US: Heat combines with pressure swings sampled near Denver, Salt Lake City, and Boise. Some people may notice lower exercise tolerance or head/sinus pressure.
- Andean South America: Pressure swings with rapid temperature changes were sampled near Quito, Lima, and La Paz. Some people may notice head or sinus pressure.
- East Asia: Poor air quality and some pressure swings were sampled near Beijing, Seoul, and Tokyo. Breathing irritation or headache may be part of what some people notice.

Space Watch
Daily activity reached a moderate background (daily Kp peak near 3.7 with southerly Bz dips), while current conditions are quieter (Kp around 1, Bz near -1.7, solar wind ~433 km/s). A catalog count of CMEs alone does not establish any Earth-directed impact.

Earth Signal
Schumann resonance fundamentals were detected near 7.37 Hz with harmonics around 14.37 and 21.83 Hz. The ultra-low-frequency geomagnetic field remained quiet, with measurements coherently detected at North American stations BOU and CMO.

Major Events
- France: Orange forest fire notification (active).
- Philippines: M4.9–M5 range earthquake, 34 km WSW of Sarangani (yellow).

Full report: gaiaeyes.com
Personalized patterns: gaiaeyes.com/app
platform_copy.instagram: Feeling the air turn heavy or switch quickly? Today highlights heat with rapid shifts in the Mid-Atlantic and Ohio–Tennessee, and a heat–pressure mix across the Mountain West. Andean South America sees sharp pressure and temperature changes, while East Asia contends with poor air and some pressure movement. Space weather runs moderate overall but is quiet right now. Earth’s resonances are steady and the ULF field is quiet. Full daily report: gaiaeyes.com
platform_copy.voiceover: If the air feels heavy or flips fast, today’s patterns back it up. Heat with quick shifts leads in the Mid-Atlantic and Ohio–Tennessee, the Mountain West mixes heat and pressure swings, Andean South America has sharp pressure and temperature changes, and East Asia faces poor air with some pressure movement. Space weather is moderate overall but quiet now, and Earth’s resonances are steady with a quiet ULF field.
